---
title: "Curriculum vitae"
subtitle: "Robert (Ross) Wardrup · Centennial, CO"
description: "Curriculum vitae for Ross Wardrup — spatial and computational epidemiology, public-health GIS, and applied statistical software."
---

::: {.tags .project-tags}
[Spatial epidemiology]{.tag} [Bayesian modeling]{.tag} [Public-health GIS]{.tag} [Surveillance data]{.tag} [SQL / R / Python]{.tag}
:::

[ross@rwardrup.com](mailto:ross@rwardrup.com) ·
[github.com/minorsecond](https://github.com/minorsecond) ·
[linkedin.com/in/robert-wardrup](https://www.linkedin.com/in/robert-wardrup/) ·
[ORCID 0009-0005-8235-6047](https://orcid.org/0009-0005-8235-6047) ·
[Google Scholar](https://scholar.google.com/citations?user=NAcxI4IAAAAJ)

I'm a researcher in spatial and computational epidemiology and an MPH
Epidemiology candidate, with a background in medical geography and more than a
decade of quantitative and geospatial work. My research applies Bayesian spatial
models and cluster-detection methods to infectious and vector-borne disease
surveillance, with an emphasis on validating those models before I trust them.
That work is backed by hands-on experience across public-health surveillance,
healthcare analytics, and federal hazard modeling, so I build the pipelines and
models myself.

## Research interests

Spatial and computational epidemiology; Bayesian spatial and spatiotemporal
modeling; infectious and vector-borne disease; environmental health; health
disparities; maternal and child health; health services research.

## Education

**Master of Public Health (MPH), Epidemiology**\
University of Memphis · *in progress, Aug 2022 – present (expected 2027)*

Coursework: Epidemiology I & II, Biostatistics I & II, Environmental
Epidemiology, Spatial Epidemiology, Epidemiologic Methods, Survey Research
Methods, Causal Inference, Environmental Health.

**Bachelor of Science, Geography**\
University of North Texas, Denton, TX · *May 2013*

## Thesis

**Bayesian spatiotemporal modeling of West Nile virus, California.**
A pooled-prevalence, BYM2-analog hierarchical model of WNV vector positivity
across California counties, 2006–2024, with distributed-lag climate covariates
and queen-contiguity spatial structure. Since the surveillance data is
restricted pending IRB approval, I'm validating the approach first on simulated
data, checking interval coverage across model specifications and scenarios.
[Project page](projects/wnv-thesis.qmd)

## Public-health experience

**MPH Epidemiology Practicum Intern**\
Southeast Utah Health Department · *May 2026 – present, Remote / Price, UT*

- Analyzed 3,454 respiratory-illness records (2015–2026) with ArcGIS Pro / Python,
  Getis-Ord Gi\*, and Emerging Hot Spot Analysis to describe spatial and temporal
  disease burden across the jurisdiction.
- Produced reusable surveillance maps, method notes, and briefing materials used
  in department planning, including mobile-vaccine-clinic planning.
- Built EHR-based (eClinicalWorks / eBO) immunization-compliance logic for
  school-entry requirements: CVX-to-requirement mapping, age-gated criteria,
  distinct-dose counting, and missing-dose flags.
- Identified a report-duplication issue in the EHR export that was inflating
  vaccine dose counts, and documented QA to keep it out of outreach before
  correction.

**Epidemiology & Data Science Professional (Temp)**\
Tri-County Health Department · *Jan – May 2021, Remote*

- Built ETL workflows in FME / FME Server to integrate public-health
  surveillance datasets into centralized geospatial databases.
- Designed and maintained PostGIS databases supporting COVID-19 case
  surveillance and vaccination monitoring.
- Developed public-facing dashboards and epidemiologic visualizations for
  department staff and community stakeholders.
- Performed QA and validation of public-health datasets.

## Professional experience

**GIS Developer (Contract)**\
FEMA, via Marathon TS and IBM · *Dec 2024 – present, Remote*

- Led a 2,000-event validation study comparing two hazard-data source formats
  for a national disaster-risk model, using descriptive statistics and
  confidence intervals to quantify agreement; identified a geospatial
  data-processing defect corrected before deployment.
- Designed a Python geospatial data abstraction standardizing coordinate-
  reference handling, spatial joins, and feature-class I/O across hazard modules.
- Maintain SQL Server databases supporting federal hazard-modeling workflows,
  including scripted QA checks for reproducible model execution.

**GIS Analyst**\
DaVita Kidney Care · *May 2023 – Nov 2024, Remote*

- Built Python / ArcPy workflows supporting healthcare operations and resource
  allocation across a national dialysis-provider network.
- Conducted spatial and statistical analyses of healthcare access and
  care-delivery patterns using R and GIS tools.
- Handled extraction, transformation, integration, and validation of large
  healthcare datasets.

**Prior GIS roles.** Staff GIS Developer at Axim Geospatial (Dec 2021 – May
2023): geospatial planning and mapping application development in Python, VB.NET,
and JavaScript/TypeScript, plus QGIS data validation. GIS Engineer at Congruex /
CHC Consulting (May – Dec 2021): fiber-network infrastructure design and utility
spatial-data analysis.

## Selected projects

**MESA (Mapping Epidemiology in Sparse Areas).** C++ desktop application for
public-health spatial analysis in rural and sparse settings: geocoding,
Getis-Ord Gi\*, Moran's I, and Monte Carlo permutation testing, validated against
PySAL and R. [Details](projects/mesa.qmd)

**Spatial analysis of vaccination exemptions in Colorado** *(Nov 2023).*
Hotspot analysis and spatial interpolation (kriging, rate smoothing) of
non-medical vaccination exemptions in R, examining associations with
socioeconomic indicators.

## Skills

**Epidemiology & research.** Descriptive epidemiology; retrospective cohort
studies; surveillance analysis; health-disparities research; longitudinal data
analysis; regression modeling; Bayesian hierarchical modeling; simulation
studies and model calibration; spatial epidemiology (cluster detection with
SaTScan, Getis-Ord Gi\*, and Moran's I; spatial lag and error regression); data
validation; reproducible research.

**Programming & data.** SQL (PostgreSQL/PostGIS, T-SQL); R (tidyverse, ggplot2,
R-INLA); SAS; Python (pandas, NumPy, ArcPy); Git; ETL workflows; database
development; data QA.

**Visualization & GIS.** Tableau; ArcGIS Pro; ArcGIS Online; QGIS; FME.

## Publications

See [Publications & research](publications.qmd).

## Professional memberships

- Society for Epidemiologic Research (SER), student member, 2025–present.
- Council of State and Territorial Epidemiologists (CSTE), student member,
  2025–present.
